verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the beta subunit of mitochondrial ATP synthase (F1F0-ATPase). Its canonical localization is the inner mitochondrial membrane/matrix face. However, there is well-documented literature showing that ATP synthase subunits, including the beta subunit (also called beta-F1-ATPase), are expressed on the plasma membrane surface of various cell types including tumor cells, hepatocytes, endothelial cells, and adipocytes. On the cell surface, the ectopic beta subunit functions as a receptor for angiostatin, apolipoprotein A-I, and other ligands. This surface form is tumor/cell-state-associated and is not present on all cell types under baseline conditions, making this contextual rather than constitutive.
